column1: # MAT-2 [Low/Med] — NOMINAL VOLUME MIX per grade (cement:sand:aggregate). Value
# is a ratio string (reference for hand mass/volume batching). Only C15 carried
# one before. Designed-mix grades (C30+) are normally design-mix in practice; the
# nominal mix is the field-batching equivalent.
# MAT-2 [Low/Med] — LEAN / BLINDING CONCRETE (unreinforced; no STEEL band).
# BOQ-accuracy audit F8: reinforcement density is driven by ELEMENT TYPE
# concrete grade. These are the accepted kg-steel/m³-concrete bands (civilsir /
# kairalitmt / OneClickLCA). TypeKey sourced from element category / structural
# usage. The grade-keyed CONCRETE STEEL_KG_PER_M3 rows above remain a fallback
# proxy for when element type is unknown.

column6: Default when element type unknown (~C25/30 proxy)

column2: so a labelled 1:6 behaved like ~1:4.1 (sand under-ordered

column1: # volume/bag = 0.034722 m³. For a 1:N mix the sand:cement volume ratio is N:1
# SAND_RATIO (m³ sand per m³ mortar) = N × CEMENT_BAGS_PER_M3 × 0.034722. Cement
# bags are unchanged (they were correct). Each mix now delivers its true cement:sand.
# TypeKey sourced from: BLE_MORTAR_MIX_TXT. LIME_RATIO + SAND_RATIO are volume
# parts per 1 part cement. Lime improves workability/water retention; sand parts
# rise as the mix weakens. CEMENT_BAGS recomputed at 0.034722 m³/bag for the
# cement fraction of a ~1.25 m³ dry yield.
# TILE SIZES (waste varies by size and pattern)

column6: Large tiles (600mm+) - low waste but heavy

column1: # MAT-2 [High] — plaster/render now carry a cement:sand MIX so cement + sand are
# derivable. CEMENT_BAGS_PER_M3 / SAND_RATIO mirror the corrected MORTAR table
# (1:4 internal/render = 9 bags + 1.25 m³ sand per m³ of plaster). Cement+sand for
# a wall = plaster volume (face area × THICKNESS × (1+WASTE)) × these per-m³ values.
# LIME plaster is lime:sand (no OPC) → CEMENT_BAGS 0
# SCREED (floor screed) — MAT-2 [High] new section. Cement:sand mix + thickness.
# TypeKey sourced from: BLE_SCREED_TYPE_TXT. Cement+sand for a floor = area ×
